feedback.text: Hello thank you for taking the time to read my feedback and I would like to raise my voice to bring forth some Issues that have been happening around the world in an attempt to bring these themes forth for them to be discussed more thoroughly. I think we are currently facing an onslaught of unfair practices being pushed by foreign companies that in my opinion we can not allow to stand. Not if we want to protect the people of Europe's rights, from abuse. Among which we have the sales of digital Videogames to people, when in reality, and as most people are not fully aware off, People are not buying the right to enjoy said video games indefinitely instead only until the Companies who make them see it more profitable for them to be taken away in an act best described as destruction of ART even thought the games were legally purchased by EU consumers. This is referencing the stop Killing Games Initiative. Another issue we are currently facing is the censorship imposed by payment processors like Visa and Mastercard over the art form that is videogames where we have seen a massive overstep in an unprecedented move where there seems to have been potential threats to distribution platforms, about the content they distribute, But why should they decide that? Do I not have national laws that operates within the Law of the EU that decide what can be legally distributed? Albeit this situation seems to mostly be unfolding in the UK for now. I can not stress how serious this genre of censorship is for EU member states as all of our nations have Incredible pieces of art symbols of our cultures and ways of life, each one Iconic in its own way, that during different periods of time would have been illegal to produce or even burned to cinders but instead are on display in museums. The final issue I have that might be worth discussing is regarding Identification on the Internet forcing adults to share their personal information endangering their right to anonymity during a time period where antisemitism, racism, sexism, Homophobia, transphobia and several other types of hate are on the rise, while news of ever growing larger data breaches, of even the biggest companies keep flooding the internet. Is it not just putting people at risk? when we have to share our personal information to Companies that repeatedly have failed to protect us from information breaches? Internet Privacy is a right that needs to be protected. Using children's safety as an excuse is not good enough, when parents hold the power to surveil their children's online activities, and gate keeping dangerous websites is a feature that exist on almost every internet enabled device nowadays, Safety on the internet from things that could be harmful to children has never been easier to implement by any concerned parent and as someone who works for a tech firm Almost every company is willing to help out a concerned parent that might not be Tech Savvy on how to set up protections for their kids, and any parent who buys their child an online enabled device should have the responsibility to understand the risks of choosing to give that device to their children. Thank you very much for your time. To whoever might read this Wish you Guys all the best.
